omission_flags: The analysis omits the role of the Royal Malaysian Navy (RMN) in repeatedly changing the ship's design requirements (e.g., shifting from the Sigma to the Gowind class), which was a major contributing factor to the delays and cost overruns. Placing the entirety of the timeline blame purely on financial mismanagement avoids the technical mismanagement aspect.
evasion_flags: The analysis evades discussing whether Malaysia has any viable recourse or refund mechanism for the ~RM550m already paid to Kongsberg, focusing solely on the impossibility of suing for breach.

cardinal_sin_flags.quote: Zero convictions across 15 years of investigation.
cardinal_sin_flags.why: The LCS procurement project originated around 2011 (roughly 15 years ago), but official investigations by MACC, the PAC, and the Special Investigation Committee on Public Governance, Procurement and Finance (JKSTUPKK) only commenced after the 2018 change of government. It is factually inaccurate to claim investigations have been ongoing for 15 years.
cardinal_sin_flags.fix: Change to 'Zero convictions across the 15-year lifespan of the project' or 'Zero convictions despite years of high-level investigations'.

cardinal_sin_flags.quote: Australia, which recognised Palestine in November 2025
cardinal_sin_flags.why: While Australia's ALP government shifted its stance on Palestinian statehood and voted affirmatively on UN resolutions, a formal, definitive bilateral recognition of the State of Palestine by Australia in November 2025 requires explicit primary documentation (e.g., a formal DFAT declaration) which is not widely corroborated or is often conflated with UN assembly votes.
cardinal_sin_flags.fix: Remove the claim and stick to the alliance discriminator, or change to 'Australia, which voted to expand Palestinian rights at the UN'.

verification_log.claim: The 2022 PAC report found the programme ceiling ballooned from RM9b to RM11.145b
RM6.083 billion paid to Boustead Naval Shipyard.
RM400m of LCS funds was diverted to retire a prior naval programme's debt.
verification_log.primary_source: JKSTUPKK declassified findings via Malaysiakini 17 Aug 2022

verification_log.claim: Zero convictions across 15 years of investigation.
verification_log.primary_source: MACC / PAC timeline records (Investigations commenced post-2018)
verification_log.corrected_value: Zero convictions over the project's 15-year lifespan / 7 years of investigation.

verification_log.claim: Australia, which recognised Palestine in November 2025
verification_log.primary_source: I cannot verify this independently (Requires DFAT primary gazette for bilateral recognition, often confused with UN voting records)

overall_assessment: The analysis effectively reframes the Norwegian NSM cancellation away from a purely diplomatic grievance and toward the root domestic governance failures of the LCS project. However, it commits a significant overclaim by stating there have been '15 years of investigation' (conflating the project's age with the investigative timeline, which began post-2018) and includes an unverified geopolitical detail regarding Australia's formal recognition of Palestine. Fixing these chronological and geopolitical inaccuracies is required to ensure the piece's credibility.
